element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player control over team composition and transfers
Robust create-a-player option for roster management
Core offensive gameplay during match play
Core defensive gameplay alternating with batting
Single player game, full season, playoffs, or Home Run Derby

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based alternating action between pitcher and batter creates natural tension-relief cycles; full season and playoff modes extend pacing across multiple game sessions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible single-game mode introduces core mechanics with manageable opposition
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Full season play escalates difficulty through varied opponent teams and cumulative fatigue/morale factors
difficulty_curve.late_game: Playoff and championship modes present peak competition with highest-caliber AI teams
notable_spikes: Transition from single game to season mode (extended strategic depth)
Playoff series (elevated stakes and opponent quality)

notable_level_patterns.description: Standardized 9-inning structure with alternating offensive/defensive halves; each at-bat represents a micro-tension-relief cycle
Extended campaign mode through multiple games; cumulative record and standings create long-term tension arc
Playoff bracket and championship finals escalate narrative stakes; best-of-series format extends critical moments
Home Run Derby isolates batting mechanics in arcade-style pressure scenario; rapid-fire tension-relief through successful hits
Specialized campaign mode with distinct progression structure; introduces additional narrative/competitive framework

data_quality_notes: T3 tier - source provides gameplay features and modes but lacks detailed mechanical documentation. Rhythm and spatial patterns inferred from sports game conventions and documented mode descriptions rather than explicit design documentation.
